name: Client Acquisition Funnel
category: Traffic / Advanced — Local Service Lead Generation

goals: Generate a continuous flow of new clients/patients for a LOCAL service business or professional via paid ads to a funnel (the chiropractor example).
Replace word-of-mouth dependence with 24/7 automated client generation.
Turn ad clicks into booked appointments/consultations.
signals: Client is a local service provider or professional (medical, trades, agency, coaching with consults).
Goal is booked appointments / qualified leads, not product sales.
Client runs (or wants to run) geo-targeted paid ads.
antiSignals: Pure ecommerce/product sale -> use a tripwire/SLO/VSL funnel.
High-ticket take-away sale requiring an application + closer -> use the Application Funnel instead.

pageStructure.purpose: Match the local ad promise, establish trust fast, and drive to booking with one clear plan (StoryBrand).
elements: StoryBrand: customer-as-hero, name the problem, position the business as the guide with a plan
Social proof: reviews, star ratings, before/after results, trust badges
The offer (free consult / exam / quote / first-visit special)
Map / hours / NAP for local trust

pageStructure.purpose: Convert the lead into a booked appointment or a contact request with minimum friction.
elements: Calendar booking widget (self-schedule) OR short contact/quote form
Reassurance copy ('what happens on your visit', no-pressure)

pageStructure.purpose: Confirm the appointment, set expectations, and start the show-up + nurture automation.

copyFramework.primaryPersona: Donald Miller — StoryBrand persona (The Brand Clarifier): the SB7 framework — (1) a Character who (2) has a Problem, (3) meets a Guide, (4) who gives them a Plan, (5) and calls them to Action, (6) that ends in Success and (7) helps them avoid Failure — plus the one-liner and the grunt test.
supportingPersonas: Russell Brunson — DotCom Secrets persona (The Funnel Architect): Star/Story/Solution sales letter script, the Value Ladder, Who/What/Why/How lead logic, Soap Opera Sequence + Daily Seinfeld follow-up.
Jim Edwards — Copywriting Secrets persona (The Copy Mechanic): the Big Promise headline formulas, fascination/curiosity bullets, the order-bump and OTO close formulas, the 'one-sentence' hook.
scripts: StoryBrand SB7 — make the local customer the hero, the business the guide, give a 3-step plan, show success vs the cost of inaction.
DotCom Secrets lead-funnel Who/What/Why/How logic for the landing structure.
Jim Edwards local Big-Promise headline + offer copy (free consult/exam/quote).
copyFramework.notes: Clarity beats cleverness for local buyers. Lead with the city + outcome, prove it with local reviews/before-after, and make booking a single obvious action. The plan must feel safe and easy.

pages: Build as a GHL Funnel: Step 1 Landing -> Step 2 Booking -> Step 3 Confirmation.
Set GHL page SEO + ad URL params for geo/campaign tracking.
skill44Widgets: Skill 44 CALENDAR widget on Step 2 (GHL Calendar) for self-scheduling consults/appointments.
Skill 44 Form widget alternative for quote/contact capture when self-booking isn't suitable.
Skill 44 Form for lead qualification fields.
automations: Workflow: booking -> confirmation email/SMS + appointment reminder sequence (cut no-shows).
Workflow: contact-form lead -> instant speed-to-lead SMS + staff notification + nurture.
ghlBuild.notes: Skill 6 builds the 3-step funnel; Skill 44 instantiates the GHL Calendar booking widget (the core conversion asset) and/or the contact form. Speed-to-lead automation is essential for local lead-gen — wire the instant-text workflow.
